issues: The average volume of -16.6 dB is within the optimal range for speech transcription, providing excellent audibility without introducing excessive noise floor. No gain adjustments or normalization are required.
No digital clipping was detected, preserving the integrity of the waveform and preventing harmonic distortion. This ensures high-fidelity audio that will maximize transcription accuracy.

The silence ratio of 13.4% indicates a natural conversational flow with standard pauses and no significant dead air. The audio is ready for transcription without the need for pre-processing or silence truncation.
The average volume of -25.3 dB is significantly low and may impair transcription accuracy. Recommend boosting the gain by approximately 6 to 8 dB to achieve an optimal speech listening level.
No clipping is detected in the signal, which is excellent for audio fidelity. This provides sufficient headroom to safely increase the volume without introducing digital distortion.

A silence ratio of 1.2% indicates almost continuous audio throughout the recording. Editors should verify if this is due to constant speech rather than persistent background hum or static that requires noise gating.
